title: Brazil Amazon enforcement rollback (2019–2022)
text: Reduced IBAMA enforcement budgets and fines, weakened environmental licensing, and signalled tolerance for land clearing in the Legal Amazon. Primarily an administrative/economic policy shift rather than an explicit land-use law.
observedDelta: Sentinel-2 / PRODES show annual primary-forest loss rising from ~9,760 km² (2019) to ~13,038 km² (2021); dry-season FIRMS fire counts and Sentinel-5P CO spiked over the same window.

title: Indonesia permanent peatland & forest moratorium (2016→2019)
text: Extended and later made permanent a moratorium on new concessions in primary forest and peatland, plus a peatland restoration agency. Mixes land-use regulation with agricultural-subsidy and concession-economics levers.
observedDelta: MODIS/VIIRS active-fire counts and AOD over peat provinces fell sharply after 2016 vs the 2015 haze crisis; NBR burn-severity deltas show fewer large peat burns in subsequent dry seasons.

title: BC FireSmart & fuel-management funding (2018→)
text: Sustained provincial funding for community fuel-reduction, prescribed burns, and wildfire prevention. An economic/budget lever whose climate-adjacent effect is mediated through fuel loads.
observedDelta: Landsat NBR in treated wildland-urban-interface units shows lower burn severity where fuel treatments preceded fire; treated-vs-untreated pairs visible in post-2021 season imagery.

title: Ontario forest-management & conservation budget cuts (2019)
text: Reduced conservation-authority funding and forest-management program spending. A purely economic policy with no explicit climate language, but with downstream effects on fuel management and monitoring capacity.
observedDelta: Reduced prescribed-burn and thinning activity correlates with higher modelled fuel accumulation; FIRMS shows sustained boreal fire activity, though attribution to the cuts alone is weak and flagged as such.
